sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("=== Key objects ===")
for n in nodes:
    c = n['color']
    if c in ['W', 'p', 'M', 'G'] and n['pixels'] <= 25:
        if n['boundary']:
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            center = f"~({sum(rows)//len(rows)}, {sum(cols)//len(cols)})"
        else:
            center = "?"
        print(f"  {c} id={n['id']} px={n['pixels']} children={n['children']} {center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- p@(33, 34) with M child - purple square in the middle

Looking at the image, the white diamond is at the bottom, the purple square is in the middle, and the dark gray square is at the top (where the ring was). The ring is now a dark gray square!
So the sequence is:
1. White diamond at ring position
2. Click purple square → white diamond moves there
3. Click dark gray square → white diamond swaps with it
4. Now white diamond is at bottom, dark gray square is at top (ring position)
But the level is not complete. I need to figure out what to do next. Maybe I need to click the purple square again to swap them back, then click the dark gray square (now at the ring position)?
